prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Georgia joined the war in Afghanistan in 2004 and the country had become the largest non-NATO and the largest per capita troop contributor to the International Security Assistance Force in Afghanistan by late 2012, with over 1,560 personnel on the ground as of May 2013. At its peak deployment, Georgia provided two full infantry battalions serving with the United States forces in the Helmand province. Since the beginning of their mission, more than 11,000 Georgian soldiers have served in Afghanistan.The Georgian mission in Helmand ended in July 2014. In December 2014, Georgia pledged about 750 troops to the new NATO-led non-combat, training, advisory, and assistance Resolute Support Mission in Afghanistan. At various times, the country has also deployed an infantry company serving with the French contingent in Kabul,
B. Georgia joined the war in Afghanistan in 2004 and by the end of 2012, it had become the largest contributor to the International Security Assistance Force in Afghanistan, with over 1,560 personnel on the ground. At its peak, Georgian soldiers served with the United States forces in Helmand province. Since the start of their mission, over 11,000 Georgian soldiers have served in Afghanistan. In July 2014, the Georgian mission in Helmand ended. In December 2014, Georgia pledged about 750 troops to the new NATO-led non-combat, training, advisory
C. Beginning in 2004, Georgia became involved in the conflict in Afghanistan, and by the latter part of 2012, it had risen to the position of the foremost non-NATO nation and the most significant per capita contributor of troops to the International Security Assistance Force stationed in Afghanistan. By May 2013, the country maintained a presence of more than 1,560 military personnel within the country. During its most extensive deployment, Georgia supplied a force comprising two complete infantry battalions, which operated alongside United States military units within the Helmand province. Throughout the duration of their engagement, a total exceeding 11,000 Georgian military personnel participated in service in Afghanistan. The Georgian deployment in Helmand concluded in July 2014. Subsequently, in December 2014, Georgia committed approximately 750 soldiers to the newly established NATO-led Resolute Support Mission in Afghanistan, which focused on non-combat, training, advisory, and assistance roles. On several occasions, an infantry company from the country was also deployed, serving alongside the French contingent in Kabul.

Answer:

options.A: Georgia joined the war in Afghanistan in 2004 and the country had become the largest non-NATO and the largest per capita troop contributor to the International Security Assistance Force in Afghanistan by late 2012, with over 1,560 personnel on the ground as of May 2013. At its peak deployment, Georgia provided two full infantry battalions serving with the United States forces in the Helmand province. Since the beginning of their mission, more than 11,000 Georgian soldiers have served in Afghanistan.The Georgian mission in Helmand ended in July 2014. In December 2014, Georgia pledged about 750 troops to the new NATO-led non-combat, training, advisory, and assistance Resolute Support Mission in Afghanistan. At various times, the country has also deployed an infantry company serving with the French contingent in Kabul,
options.B: Georgia joined the war in Afghanistan in 2004 and by the end of 2012, it had become the largest contributor to the International Security Assistance Force in Afghanistan, with over 1,560 personnel on the ground. At its peak, Georgian soldiers served with the United States forces in Helmand province. Since the start of their mission, over 11,000 Georgian soldiers have served in Afghanistan. In July 2014, the Georgian mission in Helmand ended. In December 2014, Georgia pledged about 750 troops to the new NATO-led non-combat, training, advisory
options.C: Beginning in 2004, Georgia became involved in the conflict in Afghanistan, and by the latter part of 2012, it had risen to the position of the foremost non-NATO nation and the most significant per capita contributor of troops to the International Security Assistance Force stationed in Afghanistan. By May 2013, the country maintained a presence of more than 1,560 military personnel within the country. During its most extensive deployment, Georgia supplied a force comprising two complete infantry battalions, which operated alongside United States military units within the Helmand province. Throughout the duration of their engagement, a total exceeding 11,000 Georgian military personnel participated in service in Afghanistan. The Georgian deployment in Helmand concluded in July 2014. Subsequently, in December 2014, Georgia committed approximately 750 soldiers to the newly established NATO-led Resolute Support Mission in Afghanistan, which focused on non-combat, training, advisory, and assistance roles. On several occasions, an infantry company from the country was also deployed, serving alongside the French contingent in Kabul.
